text: Lesson. Ordering coffee. Beginner. Here is your quick 20-second lesson on how to grab your morning caffeine fix like a pro. The dialogue barista. Hi there, what can I get for you? Customer. Can I have a large latte, please? Barista. Sure. Anything else today? Customer. No thanks, that's all. Barista. Great. That'll be $5 or dollars. Vocabulary word meaning latte. A coffee drink made with espresso and steamed milk. Large. The big size. Usually 16 to 20 oz. That's all of phrase used to finish your order. Quick tips for natural English. Can I have is the most common way to start an order. It's polite but casual. Hi there is a very common friendly greeting you'll hear in most shops. Instead of saying, I am finished, always use that's all or that's it when you reach the end of your list.

segments.text: Here is your quick 20-second lesson on how to grab your morning caffeine fix like a pro.
Hi there, what can I get for you?
Can I have a large latte, please?
A coffee drink made with espresso and steamed milk.
That's all of phrase used to finish your order.
Can I have is the most common way to start an order.
Hi there is a very common friendly greeting you'll hear in most shops.
Instead of saying, I am finished, always use that's all or that's it when you reach the end of your list.
